question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the totalDurationMetric.record() call in parse() which uses streamFile.getLoadEnd() - streamFile.getLoadStart(), so that if loadEnd or loadStart is null (not set on the RecordFile), a NullPointerException is thrown after doParse() but before doFlush(), causing onEnd() to never be called and all transactions to be suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the parseLatencyMetric.record() call that computes Duration.between(consensusInstant, Instant.now()) using streamFile.getConsensusEnd(), so that a crafted RecordFile with a far-future consensusEnd causes an exception or metric overflow that prevents doFlush() from completing and suppresses all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on RecordFileParser.parse() to perform a denial-of-service by holding the lock indefinitely (e.g., by causing a deadlock inside onEnd()), preventing any other thread from calling parse() and suppressing all subsequent transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SqlEntityListener.onEnd() calls flush() which iterates over the ParserContext via context.forEach(batchPersister::persist), so that if a crafted transaction causes batchPersister.persist() to throw a non-ParserException, it is wrapped in a new ParserException and re-thrown, rolling back the entire transaction and suppressing all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with a batchKey set but a null parentConsensusTimestamp, causing SqlEntityListener.onTransaction() to call Utility.handleRecoverableError() which may throw an exception that propagates through onEnd() and prevents the flush from completing, suppressing all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NFT_FLUSH path in SqlEntityListener.onNftTransferList() where flushNftState() is called mid-processing, so that if flushNftState() throws an exception, the ParserContext is left in a partially flushed state and the subsequent onEnd() flush persists inconsistent data or suppresses some transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener iterates listeners by index (for int i = 0; i < listeners.size(); i++) without catching exceptions between iterations, so that a RuntimeException from listener i causes listeners i+1 through N to never receive onEnd(), suppressing their respective transaction flushes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpEL-based transaction filter (include/exclude configuration) by crafting a transaction whose transactionBody.memo or other field matches an exclude filter, causing the transaction to be silently dropped before onEnd() is called, effectively preventing that transaction from being gossiped to the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpEL expression evaluation in transaction filtering to inject a denial-of-service expression (e.g., an infinite loop or a very expensive computation) that causes the filter evaluation to hang, preventing onEnd() from ever being reached and suppressing all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.EMPTY sentinel value has null consensusEnd and null hash, so that if EMPTY is somehow passed to onEnd() via a code path that doesn't validate the file, the flush() in SqlEntityListener persists nothing and all real transactions are suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile's items list is a @Transient field not persisted to the database, so that if onEnd() is called after items are cleared (e.g., due to a race condition with streamFile.clear()), the listeners receive a RecordFile with an empty items list and cannot reconstruct which transactions were processed, leading to silent suppression?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the hash field in RecordFile is derived from the endObjectRunningHash, so that by crafting a record file with a valid but manipulated endObjectRunningHash, the hash chain appears valid to shouldParse() but the actual transaction content is different, causing onEnd() to persist forged transactions while suppressing the real ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the metadataHash field (which is @Transient and not persisted) to bypass signature verification in the downloader, allowing a crafted record file to pass verification and reach onEnd() with forged transactions that suppress legitimate ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.index (block number) is not validated in the onEnd() path, so that a crafted file with a duplicate or out-of-order index causes the streamFileRepository to overwrite an existing record, suppressing the transactions from the legitimate file with that index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.gasUsed field defaults to 0L and is not validated, so that a crafted file with an extremely large gasUsed value causes an arithmetic overflow in metric recording after onEnd(), triggering an exception that rolls back the transaction and suppresses all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.logsBloom field is a @Transient byte array, so that if a crafted file provides an extremely large logsBloom value, it causes an OutOfMemoryError during onEnd() processing (which is excluded from @Retryable), permanently suppressing all transactions in that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.name field is used in log messages and exception messages within onEnd() processing, so that a crafted file with a specially formatted name (e.g., containing format specifiers or extremely long strings) causes a logging exception that prevents onEnd() from completing and suppresses all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.consensusStart and consensusEnd are Long values, so that a crafted file with consensusStart > consensusEnd causes an invalid time range that confuses the shouldParse() check and causes subsequent legitimate files to be skipped, suppressing their transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.bytes field is cleared by streamFile.clear() after onEnd(), so that if a listener in CompositeRecordStreamFileListener attempts to access the bytes field after a previous listener has triggered clear(), it receives null bytes and cannot validate the file content, leading to silent acceptance of forged transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.fileHash is a separate field from hash (the running hash), so that a crafted file where fileHash and hash are inconsistent passes the hash chain check (which uses hash) but fails a separate integrity check, causing an exception in onEnd() that suppresses all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.sidecarCount field is not validated against the actual number of sidecars in the items list, so that a crafted file with a mismatched sidecarCount causes an exception during sidecar processing in onEnd(), rolling back the transaction and suppressing all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecordFile.digestAlgorithm field is an enum that can be null, so that if a crafted file has a null digestAlgorithm, the hash computation in onEnd() throws a NullPointerException that prevents the flush from completing and suppresses all transactions?
